2605.27249.title: Gumbel Machine: Counterfactual Student Writing Generation via Gumbel Noise Steering
2605.27249.paper_summary: An effective method of teaching across disciplines is to provide examples of high-quality work. However, an example may be significantly different from a student's current work, making it challenging for them to emulate. An ideal learning demonstration is a counterfactual version of the student work, an improved version that is still similar to their own. Existing automated approaches for counterfactual text generation using Large Language Models (LLMs) result in domain-specific systems that are difficult to translate into practical applications. We present the Gumbel Machine, a flexible, modular approach to generating counterfactuals that leverages LLM instruction-following capabilities while encouraging similarity to a reference factual text. Central to our approach is a novel, controlled decoding algorithm, $β$-Hindsight control, which uses latent randomness as a tunable similarity control mechanism during counterfactual generation. Experiments on datasets of student writing, scored on various criteria, demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach at generating counterfactuals both rubric-consistent and similar to a reference.
2605.27249.paper_summary_zh: 一種有效的跨學科教學方法是提供高品質工作的範例。<lb/>然而，範例可能與學生目前的工作有顯著差異，使他們難以模仿。<lb/>理想的學習示範是學生工作的反事實版本，即一個改進的版本，但仍然與他們的作品相似。<lb/>現有的自動化反事實文本生成方法使用大型語言模型（LLMs），導致領域特定的系統，這些系統難以轉化為實際應用。<lb/>我們提出了Gumbel Machine，一種靈活的模組化生成反事實的方法，利用LLM的指令跟隨能力，同時鼓勵與參考事實文本的相似性。<lb/>我們方法的核心是一種新穎的受控解碼算法，$β$-Hindsight控制，該算法在反事實生成過程中使用潛在隨機性作為可調的相似性控制機制。<lb/>在學生寫作的數據集上進行的實驗，根據各種標準進行評分，展示了我們的方法在生成既符合評分標準又與參考相似的反事實方面的有效性。
2605.27249.author: Hunter McNichols et.al.
2605.27249.authors: Hunter McNichols, Alexander Scarlatos, Mihai Dascalu, Danielle McNamara, Andrew Lan
